**Activate—Mirror Mimicry** <actions string="" /> 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645), [occult](/Traits.aspx?ID=662))    **Effect** The mirror transforms its owner's appearance into an exact copy of any [humanoid](/Traits.aspx?ID=628) the owner desires, with a pale mimicry of that creature's abilities. This has the effects of a 3rd-rank [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1568) spell with a duration of 4 hours. The activation can also be Dismissed.

 In addition, the disguised creature can automatically create illusions to mimic the abilities of the subject, with the appearance of spells, abilities, or even impressive physical deeds. These deeds are entirely illusory and can be disbelieved with a successful DC 28 Perception check. If an illusion makes it appear as though the creature moved farther or differently than they actually can (such as making them fly or teleport), the actual creature turns [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), and their illusory image persists until the end of the creature's next turn. If the creature and their illusory self aren't in the same space at that point, the activation ends, revealing the deception.
name: 10 minutes (concentrate, manipulate, occult)

markdown: **Activate—Rule by Roots** <actions string="Three Actions" /> ([fungus](/Traits.aspx?ID=614), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per week;   **Effect** You plunge the _Cythbikian staff_ into the earth, causing a vast fungal network to spread out from the staff, coating the ground, plants, structures, and all creatures in a 100- foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) (DC 34 Reflex avoids) until the staff is removed or 24 hours have passed. Non-magical plants in the area die. A creature covered by these fungal growths is [grabbed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=77) and [controlled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=64), physically, by the fungus, as it moves them around like a living puppet. At the beginning of a grabbed creature’s turn, they can attempt to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) (DC 34). On a failure, the fungus forces them to spend all of their remaining actions moving toward and attacking the nearest creature in the area using melee [Strikes](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with any weapon at their disposal. The fungal growths pay no mind to how the bodies under their control operate, and grabbed creatures are twisted, bent, and contorted in ways that harm their body. Each round, at the end of a grabbed creature’s turn, they take 8d6 bludgeoning damage from this painful manipulation (DC 34 basic Fortitude save). A creature that successfully Escapes or is physically removed from the area is no longer grabbed or controlled. When the _Cythbikian staff_ is removed from the ground, or after 24 hours have passed, the mold dies and this effect ends. Over the course of a week, the mold decays and fertilizes the soil, providing valuable nutrients to the soil and preparing the region for new plant growth.

**Activate—Unfold** <actions string="" /> 1 minute ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You unfold the triangle on near-invisible seams and the bird animates, flying up to perch in a tree you designate within 300 feet of you. The bird’s perch can be as high as 200 feet off the ground or the top of the trees, whichever is lower. The bird then alights, expands, and reshapes into an elegantly crafted tree house over the course of 10 minutes. Unlike typical items with the [structure](/Traits.aspx?ID=704) trait, this tree house attaches to the tree instead of needing to be on solid ground. 

Once the tree house is in place, a ladder made of intricately woven vines attached to the tree house unfurls all the way to the ground. It can be manually pulled up by inhabitants of the tree house for safety. The tree house has a small veranda, circular windows, a door with a simple lock, and six bedrolls inside. Atop a small cushion sits a wooden whistle. 

As an action, you can blow the whistle to create a soft bird call. At this signal, the tree house gradually folds back into a bird over the course of 1 minute, gently ejecting any occupants toward the ladder. Afterward, the bird flies to you, seizes the whistle, and then folds back into the wooden triangle. Unless commanded to dismantle, the tree house stays in place indefinitely, though it’s subject to normal wear and damage.

<traits>
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=561" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Spore War Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=252) pg. 13

</column>

---

Designate a single creature that you can see or hear, or whom you've spent a significant amount of time studying and reading about from afar (subject to the GM's approval), and who you know to be an agent of [Treerazer](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3218). You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) or [Sense the Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) of this creature. This creature takes a –1 circumstance penalty to saving throws against spells or effects you create; this penalty increases to –2 against effects that have the [holy](/Traits.aspx?ID=517) trait.

 You can have only one creature designated by Hunt the Razer's Pawn at a time. If you use this ability against a creature when you already have one designated, the prior creature loses the designation and the new creature gains the designation. This lasts until your next daily preparations.
name: Hunt the Razer's Pawn

  • **4th** [_clear mind_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1469), [_planar tether_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1636)
  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_divine wrath_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1499)

  • **7th** [_divine decree_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1495), [_planar seal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1635)

Thereafter, anyone who touches the _Guiding Star Orb_ while casting a spell with the teleportation trait to travel to this location arrives precisely, without any inaccuracy at all. markdown: **Activate—Adaptation** <actions string="Two Actions" /> ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You alter the exterior of the armor to better adapt to the surrounding [terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2365): [aquatic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2770), [arctic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2773), [desert](/Rules.aspx?ID=2776), [forest](/Rules.aspx?ID=2779), [mountain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2783), [plains](/Rules.aspx?ID=2789), sky, [swamp](/Rules.aspx?ID=2792), or [underground](/Rules.aspx?ID=2808). You [ignore difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2367) within the chosen [environment](/Rules.aspx?ID=590) and gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [environmental hazards](/Rules.aspx?ID=2588), [natural disasters](/Rules.aspx?ID=2823), and extreme temperatures that originate from that terrain. You’re also protected from [severe and extreme heat or severe and extreme cold](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820) (your choice when you activate this ability). This effect lasts until your next daily preparations or the next time you activate it, whichever comes first.

**Activate—Auroral Shine** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640)) **Frequency** once per minute; **Effect** _Wintershot_ can’t use this activation. **Failure** As success, but the creature also takes 6d6 [persistent cold damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86), and the lights affect the creature for 1 minute.
**Critical Failure** As success, but the creature also takes 6d6 persistent cold damage, and the lights affect the creature for 10 minutes.

markdown: **Activate—Signal Flare** <actions string="Single Action" /> ([light](/Traits.aspx?ID=640), [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** _Wintershot_ fires an arrow-shaped construct of ice straight upward. The arrow soars to a height of 500 feet, or until it strikes a solid surface like a ceiling. When it reaches its apex, it explodes in a brilliant burst, creating a 100-foot radius area of [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) and [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in the next 100 feet. **Activate—Rimecrust** ([cold](/Traits.aspx?ID=555), [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561), [incapacitation](/Traits.aspx?ID=631)) **Effect** _Wintershot_ causes the deep chill of winter to settle upon a single creature she can see or hear within 30 feet. That creature must attempt a DC 43 Fortitude save, after which point they’re temporarily immune to Rimecrust for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** The creature’s discomfort and shivering from the sudden drop in temperature causes it to become [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) until the start of its next turn. Creatures that are [immune](/Rules.aspx?ID=2312) to cold are immune to this effect.
**Failure** The creature becomes encrusted with ice. It becomes [slowed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=92) 1 and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 minute, or until it [Escapes](/Actions.aspx?ID=2296) from the crusting ice (DC 43).
**Critical Failure** The creature is slowed 1 and [restrained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=90) by crusting ice until it Escapes (DC 43). After it Escapes, it remains slowed 1 and [off-guard](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) for 1 minute from the lingering cold.
name: (cold, concentrate, incapacitation)

The garden’s plants actively move and twist to oppose passage by your enemies, and they treat the terrain as greater [difficult terrain](/Rules.aspx?ID=2366). The garden sanctuary’s spells are cast at 9th-rank, and it has 3 spell slots to cast spells each day. It can cast [_field of life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1526), [_moment of renewal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1607), or [_tree of seasons_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1725), treating its center as the point of origin for these spells as needed. The garden sanctuary persists until you use this activation again.

**Source** [Shining Kingdoms](/Sources.aspx?ID=265) pg. 123 **Frequency** once per hour --- Your gray bladeling attempts to inject a malevolent soul fragment into a victim to seize control of their actions. The gray bladeling makes a blade [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306) against a living creature. If it hits and deals damage, the target must attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. Regardless of the result, the target is temporarily immune to Possessing Strike for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target is unaffected.
**Success** The target fights off the possessing soul fragment but is [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=93) 1.
**Failure** The target is [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round and can’t use offensive actions against you or your gray bladeling.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but if the target takes damage from an attack or spell, the DC of the [flat check](/Rules.aspx?ID=333) to recover from the confusion is 15.
name: Possessing Strike

markdown:
**Activate—Change of Seasons** 10 minutes ([concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=561)) **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You arrange the shawl on your shoulders to gain the benefits of a season of your choice until your next daily preparations. If the season you choose is the current season, you also gain a +1 item bonus to Fortitude saving throws.
  • **Spring** The shawl becomes smooth silk covered in delicate flowers. You gain a +5-foot item bonus to your Speed.
  • **Summer** The shawl becomes light cotton with the hues of fresh grass. You’re protected from the effects of [severe heat](/Rules.aspx?ID=2820).
  • **Fall** The shawl becomes thin leather with an ombre of red and orange leaves. You treat [falls](/Rules.aspx?ID=2352) as 10 feet shorter.
  • **Winter** The shawl becomes warm wool as white as snow. You’re protected from the effects of severe cold.
name: 10 minutes (concentrate)
